acceptance_criteria: Prove list-compilation composition for compileStmts by structural induction.
Strengthen append theorem so it can be instantiated from any initial CompileState.
Add a non-trivial semantic-preservation theorem artifact for a supported compilation subset without weakening obligations.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to the supported letVar/assignVar/setStorage(localVar) subset with explicit field-resolution assumptions.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses add(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses sub(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to an arithmetic subset where assignVar rebinding uses mul(localVar,tmpLiteral) before setStorage.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a return subset where `return (literal n)` preserves source halting semantics.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a let-then-return subset where `letVar tmp (literal n); return (localVar tmp)` preserves source variable-binding and halting semantics.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a branch subset where `ite (eq (literal n) (literal m))` selects between two `setStorage` literal writes under explicit field-resolution assumptions.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (eq (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (lt (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (gt (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.
Extend semantic-preservation coverage to a require subset where `require (ge (literal n) (literal m)) message` preserves source guard/revert behavior.

theorems.name: compileStmts_append
theorems.statement_summary: Compiling pre ++ post is equivalent to sequentially compiling pre then post.

theorems.name: compileStmts_append_from
theorems.statement_summary: The append-composition theorem holds when run from any initial CompileState.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_setStorage_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for setStorage literal emits the expected typed setStorage write under explicit field-resolution assumptions.

theorems.name: compile_setStorage_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported setStorage-literal subset, compiled typed-IR execution matches direct source-level storage update semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Two-statement compilation for letVar-literal followed by setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported letVar/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics and records the expected SSA variable binding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-literal; setStorage-localVar emits the expected two SSA lets and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported letVar/assignVar/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics and records the expected SSA rebinding sequence.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_add_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-add(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed addition and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_add_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-add/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n + m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_sub_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-sub(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed subtraction and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_sub_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-sub/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n - m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_assign_mul_literal_setStorage_local_run
theorems.statement_summary: Three-statement compilation for letVar-literal; assignVar-mul(localVar,literal); setStorage-localVar emits the expected SSA let rebinding with typed multiplication and typed setStorage sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_assign_mul_setStorage_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported arithmetic letVar/assignVar-mul/setStorage-local subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source storage-update semantics (n * m) and the expected SSA rebinding effect.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_return_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for return literal emits the expected typed returnUint statement from any initial compile state.

theorems.name: compile_return_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported return-literal subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source return semantics (state unchanged, halting).

theorems.name: compileStmts_let_return_local_literal_run
theorems.statement_summary: Two-statement compilation for letVar-literal; return-localVar emits the expected SSA let and typed returnUint sequence from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_let_return_local_literal_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported let-then-return subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source variable-binding and halting semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_ite_eq_setStorage_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for ite(eq(literal,literal)) with setStorage literal branches emits the expected typed if_ with typed setStorage branches from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_ite_eq_setStorage_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported branch subset with an equality guard over literals, compiled typed-IR execution preserves source branch-selected storage update semantics.

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_eq_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(eq(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_eq_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-eq-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged on true, revert on false).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_lt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(lt(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_lt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-lt-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n < m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_gt_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(gt(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with guard lt(rhs,lhs) and a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_gt_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-gt-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n > m, revert otherwise).

theorems.name: compileStmts_single_require_ge_literals_run
theorems.statement_summary: Single-statement compilation for require(ge(literal,literal), message) emits the expected typed if_ with guard not(lt(...)) and a revert else-branch from empty compile state.

theorems.name: compile_require_ge_literals_semantics
theorems.statement_summary: For the supported require-ge-literals subset, compiled typed-IR execution preserves direct source require semantics (state unchanged when n >= m, revert otherwise).

tests: TypedIRTests: example instantiating compileStmts_append_from over arbitrary fields/pre/post/state
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_setStorage_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_setStorage_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/tmp/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_assign_setStorage_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/tmp/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_assign_add_setStorage_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/tmp/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_assign_sub_setStorage_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/tmp/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_assign_mul_setStorage_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/tmp/slot assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_return_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/init/n
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_let_return_local_literal_semantics over arbitrary fields/tmp/init/n
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_ite_eq_setStorage_literals_semantics over arbitrary fields/fieldName/slot/init/n/m/thenVal/elseVal assumptions
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_require_eq_literals_semantics over arbitrary fields/init/n/m/message
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_require_lt_literals_semantics over arbitrary fields/init/n/m/message
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_require_gt_literals_semantics over arbitrary fields/init/n/m/message
TypedIRTests: example instantiating compile_require_ge_literals_semantics over arbitrary fields/init/n/m/message

2.2.notes: 2.2 remains partial: this slice extends semantic-preservation evidence with a require theorem for `require (gt (literal n) (literal m)) message`, in addition to prior require-ge/require-lt/require-eq, branch (`ite eq`), return, and arithmetic subsets, but does not yet prove full source-vs-typed semantics for the broader supported statement fragment.
